Politics.headline: NDP MP Lori Idlout crossing floor to Liberals, bringing Carney closer to majority
Politics.detail: New Democratic Party MP Lori Idlout is crossing the floor to join the Liberals, potentially setting up Prime Minister Mark Carney to secure a majority government if upcoming byelections go his way.

Politics.headline: Starmer and Badenoch spar over Iran at PMQs ahead of Mandelson documents release – UK politics live
Politics.detail: Keir Starmer and Kemi Badenoch debated the Iran war during Prime Minister's Questions as the release of Mandelson documents looms.

Politics.headline: State Department allows up to $40M to be used for evacuation flights for Americans in Mideast
Politics.detail: The State Department has authorized up to $40 million to be used for evacuation flights for Americans in the Middle East due to transportation disruptions caused by the Iran war.

Politics.headline: Qatar warns Gulf security is tied to global stability
Politics.detail: Qatar's Mohammed bin Abdulaziz Al Khulaifi warns that attacks on Gulf states risk escalation and threaten global energy security.

Politics.headline: Americans are increasingly worried about voting, new poll shows
Politics.detail: A new poll indicates that Americans are increasingly concerned about voting.

Finance & Economy.headline: War with Iran delivers another shock to the global economy
Finance & Economy.detail: The conflict is driving up energy and fertilizer prices, threatening food shortages, destabilizing fragile states, and complicating options for central banks. The Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world's oil passes, was effectively shut down after recent missile strikes.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks hold steadier as Wall Street waits for the next signal on how long war with Iran may last.
Finance & Economy.detail: Investors are closely monitoring the ongoing conflict with Iran to assess its potential impact on the economy and financial markets. Crude oil prices have been volatile, impacting stock performance as well.

Finance & Economy.headline: Two LNG cargoes headed to India, 70% of imports bypass Strait of Hormuz
Finance & Economy.detail: Amidst the ongoing conflict, India is diversifying its energy import routes to mitigate potential disruptions. The government is also urging consumers against panic booking of LPG cylinders.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global airlines hike ticket prices as Iran war sends costs soaring
Finance & Economy.detail: Airlines are increasing ticket prices in response to rising fuel costs resulting from the war with Iran. This increase reflects the broader impact of geopolitical tensions on the transportation sector.

Finance & Economy.headline: Rheinmetall forecasts sales and earnings growth to continue in fiscal year 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Rheinmetall anticipates strong sales growth and rising margin expectations for fiscal year 2026, projecting a sales increase of 40%-45% to €14.0 billion - €14.5 billion. The Group's operating result margin is expected to be around 19%.

Technology.headline: Morocco Launches “AI Made in Morocco” Initiative to Boost Industry X.0 and Health Tech
Technology.detail: The "AI Made in Morocco" initiative aims to cultivate a national innovation ecosystem and foster the creation of locally designed technological solutions, with a focus on Industry X.0 and Health Tech.

Technology.headline: Google Completes Acquisition of Wiz
Technology.detail: Google has finalized its acquisition of Wiz, a company focused on cloud security, aiming to integrate Wiz's capabilities into Google Cloud to enhance security offerings for their customers.

Technology.headline: Corning Expands AI Data Center Connectivity Portfolio with PRIZM® TMT Technology
Technology.detail: Corning has partnered with US Conec to license PRIZM® TMT optical ferrule technology, which will enable higher fiber counts in smaller spaces within data centers, enhancing Corning's high-density optical connectivity solutions for AI networks.

Technology.headline: Apple's macOS 26.3.1 Update Gets New Urgency as Studio Displays Start Shipping
Technology.detail: Apple has released macOS 26.3.1, and the update is now more crucial as Studio Displays are being shipped to customers.

Technology.headline: Warner, Kaine seeking information on AI-related job losses
Technology.detail: Senators Warner and Kaine are seeking more information on the impact of AI on the job market, noting the uncertain picture of AI's potential to disrupt or grow employment.

Science.headline: Scientists May Have Found a Pill for Sleep Apnea
Science.detail: A European clinical trial found that the drug sulthiame significantly reduced breathing interruptions in people with moderate to severe sleep apnea, with patients taking higher doses experiencing up to 47% fewer pauses in breathing and improved oxygen levels.

Science.headline: A massive asteroid hit the North Sea and triggered a 330-foot tsunami
Science.detail: Scientists have confirmed that the Silverpit Crater beneath the North Sea was formed when a roughly 160-meter asteroid struck the seabed about 43–46 million years ago, creating a massive plume and a tsunami over 100 meters high. New seismic imaging and shocked minerals in rock samples provided the proof.

Science.headline: China's major science facilities to enter new development phase
Science.detail: China's major science and technology infrastructure projects, including massive telescopes and deep underground laboratories, are entering a new phase of development during the country's 15th Five-Year Plan period (2026-2030). The country plans to continue construction and promote more efficient use of these facilities.

Science.headline: Parts of giant Nasa satellite to crash to Earth, posing low risk
Science.detail: A giant NASA satellite is expected to crash to Earth, posing a low risk. The satellite's descent is being monitored.

Science.headline: Regeneron Science Talent Search 2026 Recognizes America's Top Young Scientists
Science.detail: Connor Hill, a 17-year-old from Pennsylvania, won the top award of $250,000 in the 2026 Regeneron Science Talent Search for discovering a way to identify all the possible "noble polyhedra", receiving more than $1.8 million in awards. Regeneron is extending its title sponsorship through 2036, pledging $150 million.

Health.headline: Scientists May Have Found a Pill for Sleep Apnea
Health.detail: A European clinical trial found that the drug sulthiame significantly reduced breathing interruptions in people with moderate to severe sleep apnea, with patients taking higher doses experiencing up to 47% fewer pauses in breathing and improved oxygen levels.

Health.headline: Cannabis, cocaine and amphetamines linked to higher stroke risk
Health.detail: Research indicates a link between the use of cannabis, cocaine, and amphetamines and an elevated risk of stroke.

Health.headline: Cannabis Study Finds THC Can Create False Memories
Health.detail: A controlled experiment revealed that cannabis users were more likely to recall words that were never shown and struggled with tasks like remembering to do something later, suggesting that THC can create new memories that never happened.

Health.headline: Training game helps nurses spot fungal infections in breastfeeding
Health.detail: A training game has been developed to assist nurses in identifying fungal infections related to breastfeeding.

Health.headline: Rapid Novor receives Ontario Ministry of Health provisional license for EasyM® blood-based MRD test for multiple myeloma
Health.detail: Rapid Novor has been granted a provisional license from the Ontario Ministry of Health for its EasyM® blood-based MRD test used for multiple myeloma.

Sports.headline: Bam! Heat's Adebayo scores 83 points, 2nd only to Wilt Chamberlain in NBA history
Sports.detail: Bam Adebayo had an incredible performance, scoring 83 points for the Miami Heat in a 150-129 win over the Washington Wizards, marking the second-highest single-game point total in NBA history, only behind Wilt Chamberlain. Adebayo started strong with 31 points in the first quarter and continued his dominance throughout the game, setting league records for free throws made and attempted.

Sports.headline: Ravens nix trade for Raiders' Maxx Crosby after star pass rusher fails his physical, AP source says
Sports.detail: The Baltimore Ravens backed out of a trade for Las Vegas Raiders' Maxx Crosby due to concerns arising from his physical examination. Despite an agreement in place, the Ravens decided against the trade after evaluating Crosby's knee, which had undergone meniscus repair in January.

Sports.headline: Geno Smith returns to Jets as career comes full circle in trade with Las Vegas, AP source says
Sports.detail: Geno Smith is returning to the New York Jets in a trade with the Las Vegas Raiders, bringing his career full circle. The details of the trade were not specified in the search results.

Sports.headline: Italy stuns the US 8-6 in World Baseball Classic, leaving the Americans needing help to advance
Sports.detail: In a surprising turn of events at the World Baseball Classic, Italy defeated the United States with a score of 8-6. This loss puts the American team in a precarious position, needing assistance from other results to advance further in the competition.

Sports.headline: Canada's Hudak, Arendz each claim bronze in cross-country skiing at Milano-Cortina Paralympics
Sports.detail: At the Milano-Cortina Paralympics, Canadians Brittany Hudak and Mark Arendz both secured bronze medals in cross-country skiing events. Arendz's medal is his second at these games and 14th overall, while Hudak earned her fourth career Paralympic medal.

Entertainment.headline: Daryl Hannah Blasts 'Love Story' Portrayal of Her and JFK Jr. Romance
Entertainment.detail: Daryl Hannah is speaking out against the 'Love Story' portrayal of her past relationship with JFK Jr.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears DUI Arrest: Troubled Star's Team Exploring Treatment Plan
Entertainment.detail: Britney Spears was arrested for DUI, and her team is reportedly exploring a treatment plan for the troubled star.

Entertainment.headline: Critics' Choice Awards 2026 nominations: Black Warrant leads, Akshaye Khanna earns nod for Dhurandhar
Entertainment.detail: The Film Critics Guild and WPP Motion Entertainment announced the nominations for the 8th Critics' Choice Awards 2026, with Black Warrant leading with six nods and Akshaye Khanna earning a nomination for Dhurandhar. The awards recognize notable work across short films, feature films, web series, and documentaries.

Entertainment.headline: Daniel Radcliffe: Mandated Therapy For Child Actors Is 'A Good Idea'
Entertainment.detail: Daniel Radcliffe believes mandatory therapy for child actors is a good idea, reflecting on the pressures young performers face. He noted the importance of psychological support, especially for productions like the new 'Harry Potter' series that involve children entering the spotlight.

Entertainment.headline: Court hearing for Cher's son on charges he broke into a New Hampshire home canceled
Entertainment.detail: A court hearing for Cher's son, Elijah Allman, on charges that he broke into a New Hampshire home has been canceled after he obtained an attorney. Allman is charged with multiple offenses, including burglary and criminal mischief, following an incident on March 1st.

Crime & Justice.headline: 2 charged in attack outside Mamdani's home
Crime & Justice.detail: Two teens are charged for allegedly trying to detonate homemade bombs in a crowd outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's official residence; prosecutors allege they were inspired by ISIS.

Crime & Justice.headline: US father found guilty of murder in Apalachee school shooting in Georgia
Crime & Justice.detail: Colin Gray was accused of criminal recklessness in allowing his 14-year-old son Colt to have access to a rifle.

Crime & Justice.headline: 1,300-pound NASA satellite set to crash down to Earth today
Crime & Justice.detail: The Van Allen probe's mission was meant to last two years, but ended up going for nearly seven.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man charged with crimes against humanity and torture of Damascus demonstrators in 2011
Crime & Justice.detail: A 58-year-old man living in the UK has been charged with international crimes allegedly committed in Damascus in 2011, including murder and torture, as part of widespread attacks on civilians. It is alleged that he led a group tasked with quelling demonstrations in the suburbs of Damascus in his role in the Syrian Air Force Intelligence department.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two teens charged in attack outside NYC Mayor's Home
Crime & Justice.detail: Two teens were charged for allegedly trying to detonate homemade bombs outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home, with prosecutors stating that they were inspired by ISIS. Both suspects allegedly said their actions were inspired by ISIS, according to the New York Police Department Commissioner Jessica Tisch.

Education.headline: Key architect of NYC class size law open to giving Mamdani more time to comply
Education.detail: A key architect of the state law mandating smaller class sizes in New York City, State Sen. John Liu, indicated he's open to extending the timeline for Mayor Zohran Mamdani to comply, suggesting it would be reasonable to extend the deadline from two years to four years. The teachers union, however, does not support extending the deadline without a clear plan from the city to increase school construction to create more space for smaller classes.

Education.headline: Scottish Week: the Scottish Parliament 2026 and higher education
Education.detail: Universities Scotland has proposed a co-designed Future Skills Fund to support new, flexible, and short courses aligned with regional and national skills needs, and they emphasize the need for a sustainable funding model, modernized student support, and practical steps to enable long-term investment and skills flexibility. The implementation of the Tertiary Education and Training (Funding and Governance) (Scotland) Act is being closely watched, with concerns that exempting private providers from Fair Work requirements could create a two-tier system.

Education.headline: What A New National Talent Strategy Means For Higher Education
Education.detail: A commission recommends replacing the current federal programs for higher education and workforce training with flexible, outcomes-based talent grants, where states would design and scale workforce-aligned education and training pathways through performance agreements with the federal government. The expansion of Pell Grant eligibility to shorter-term workforce programs needs broader vision, including outcomes-based funding and a Talent Data System.

Education.headline: GSA plan would ban DEI for all federal funding recipients — including schools
Education.detail: The General Services Administration proposal comes after a similar policy from the U.S. Department of Education was blocked in courts. The proposal would ban DEI for all federal funding recipients, including schools.

Education.headline: Ohio State University president resigns over 'inappropriate relationship'
Education.detail: Kristina M. Johnson resigned as president of Ohio State University due to an inappropriate relationship. The resignation follows an investigation into the matter.

Lifestyle.headline: Five Disneyland employees hospitalized after backstage chemical incident near Star Tours causes foul odor
Lifestyle.detail: Several Disneyland cast members were hospitalized following a chemical reaction in a backstage area near Star Tours, resulting in a foul odor.

Lifestyle.headline: Clams, raw oysters recalled over possible norovirus contamination across 9 states: FDA
Lifestyle.detail: The FDA has issued a recall for clams and raw oysters due to potential norovirus contamination across nine states.

Lifestyle.headline: Monty Don warns gardeners of 'impossible' spring weed that needs 'burning'
Lifestyle.detail: Monty Don is urging gardeners to watch out for an 'impossible' weed when sprucing up their lawns for the new season.

Lifestyle.headline: Zara Tindall 'copies' Princess Kate as she wears gorgeous £1k outfit at Cheltenham
Lifestyle.detail: Zara Tindall is seen 'copying' Princess Kate as she dons a stunning £1k outfit at Cheltenham.

Lifestyle.headline: Male Pattern Baldness Caused By Gut Health? New Study Finds Surprising Link
Lifestyle.detail: A new study suggests a surprising link between gut health and male pattern baldness.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Why America is in no mood to rally around the flag
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Trump has politicized what it means to be a patriot, assigning it based on partisan loyalty rather than love of country, undermining the capacity to rally and hindering the people's desire to do so. Americans feel better about the country when the person or party they prefer is in power.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Hunger should never be a weapon
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Aid packages should focus on humanitarian access even when ongoing conflict is occurring, with guaranteed penalties for governments and militias that willfully barricade food and health services. Diplomatic involvement shouldn't overlook valid evidence that hunger is being used as leverage.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: America is fighting a war that Iran chose
Opinion/Editorial.detail: International law does not require the U.S. and its allies to endlessly endure and absorb Iranian aggression, as the U.S. military is engaged in decisive action to permanently stop Iranian attacks. The latest U.S. military attacks against Iran have been criticized by several members of Congress, who argue the Iranian threat was insufficiently imminent to justify self-defense.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The three kinds of victory in Iran
Opinion/Editorial.detail: President Donald Trump's critics claim he has taken the United States to war in Iran with no coherent endgame in mind, while some of his own skittish advisers are urging him to end the war prematurely. A patient Trump can disarm the regime, topple it — and hand the country back to its people.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Journalists risk everything because the work is so important
Opinion/Editorial.detail: When journalists are killed for reporting the news, we must fight to ensure truth does not become a casualty too.  2025 was the deadliest year on record for the press, with 129 journalists and media workers killed worldwide.

World.headline: Three ships hit in strait of Hormuz as Iran calls vessels belonging to US or allies 'legitimate targets'
World.detail: Tensions are rising in the Strait of Hormuz after three ships were attacked, with Iran declaring that vessels belonging to the U.S. or its allies are considered legitimate targets amidst the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: UN aid worker killed in DR Congo's rebel-held Goma
World.detail: A French aid worker with UNICEF was killed in the Democratic Republic of Congo after M23 rebels claimed a combat drone hit the city.

World.headline: Iran declares US-Israeli economic, banking interests in region are targets
World.detail: The IRGC released a list of offices run by top US companies with Israeli links whose technology has been used militarily, declaring them as targets.

World.headline: White House area shut down after van breaches security barricade
World.detail: The White House area was temporarily shut down after a van breached a security barricade, prompting an investigation.

World.headline: Canada raises alarm about escalating violence in Lebanon
World.detail: The Canadian government has expressed its concern over the increasing violence and attacks in Lebanon, as the conflict in the Middle East intensifies, and called for all parties to respect humanitarian law.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police Impound Over 1 000 Vehicles in Nationwide Crackdown
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe police have impounded over 1,000 vehicles in a nationwide crackdown targeting various offenses.

Zimbabwe.headline: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe Abandons Bail as Case Moves to Regional Court
Zimbabwe.detail: Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe has abandoned his bail application, and his case has been moved to the regional court.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mutasa appointed to Zanu PF Elders Council
Zimbabwe.detail: Mutasa has been appointed to the Zanu PF Elders Council.

Zimbabwe.headline: Magaya claims rape complaint withdrawn years before charges, seeks ConCourt referral
Zimbabwe.detail: Prophet Magaya claims that the rape complaint against him was withdrawn years before charges were filed and is seeking a referral to the Constitutional Court.

Zimbabwe.headline: Diaspora group demands referendum clause in 2030 Bill
Zimbabwe.detail: A diaspora group is demanding a referendum clause be included in the 2030 Bill.
